title: Calyptrogyne costatifrons

Within the family Arecaceae, Calyptrogyne costatifrons is further classified under the tribe Geonomateae. The genus Calyptrogyne is a group of palms that are native to Central and South America. Further research is required to confirm the specific classification within the tribe and genus.

intro: Calyptrogyne costatifrons, also known as "Costa's Calyptrogyne," is a species of palm tree native to Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Calyptrogyne costatifrons include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Calyptrogyne costatifrons is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in the production of various herbal extracts and supplements.
